title: Wapol Insults Hiriluk’s Symbol
description: On pages 5–8, this part focuses on wapol insults hiriluk’s symbol, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: Wapol Insults Hiriluk’s Symbol. Luffy understands the meaning of Hiriluk’s flag and refuses to let Wapol treat it like a decoration. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: That's exactly why you're just a lying poser!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Isn't something you pretend- Pirates can go around waving, even as a joke!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Oh please! You think a king like me goes around playing pirate because he wants to!?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Stop trying to hold up that eyesore any longer!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'll shoot it down however many times it takes!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: I'd like to see you try!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Blow up to smitherreens, you deluded hippo!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I don't know who this belongs to...

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: But as a flag backed by a man's convic- lion and will,

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: It's not something you can play around with!

summary: This part highlights wapol insults hiriluk’s symbol and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
